title: A productive morning
prompt: Talk about what makes a morning productive for you. Include habits, distractions, and one thing you would like to improve.

examples.title: My best mornings
examples.body:
A productive morning for me starts with a simple plan. I wake up early, drink some water, and write down the three most important things I need to do.

The biggest distraction is my phone. If I check messages too early, I lose time. I would like to improve my habit of doing exercise before work.

examples.title: Protecting the first hour
examples.body:
For me, a productive morning depends less on waking up dramatically early and more on protecting the first hour from noise. If I begin the day by reacting to messages, I immediately lose a sense of direction.

What works best is a short routine: a clear list, a quiet breakfast, and one focused task before the day becomes fragmented. The habit I still need to strengthen is resisting the false urgency of notifications.
